english: to N v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar). — γyaṅ-kúg (g.yang kug) beggar's bag of the Lamas. — γyáṅ-can (g.yang can) happy, blessed, prosperous, γyaṅ-méd (g.yang med) the contrary. — γyaṅ-yíg (g.yang yig) a written benediction Glr. — γyaṅ-lhá (g.yang lha) a deity of the Shamans, dispensing happiness Sch. — 2. gulf, abyss, gen. γyáṅ-sa (g.yang sa) also γyaṅ-γzáṅ (g.yang g.zang); ji-tsam mťo bźin γyáṅ-sa če (ji tsam mtho bzhin g.yang sa che) so high as you stand, so deep is the gulf; lus γyáṅ-du gtóṅ-ba (lus g.yang du gtong ba) to plunge, to precipitate one's self Dzl.; γyáṅ-du (g.yang du) or γyáṅ-la ltúṅ-ba (g.yang la ltung ba) to fall down Dzl.; mčóṅ-ba (mchong ba) to leap Glr.; ṅán-soṅ-gi γyáṅ-la ǰor (ngan song gi g.yang la 'jor) Pth. he totters on the brink of the abyss of hell; γyáṅ-sa-las ʽdzin-pa (g.yang sa las 'dzin pa) to snatch from the abyss, to save Thgy.; brag-γyaṅ-γzár (brag g.yang g.zar) rocky precipice Mṅg.
Mil. nt., C. a bowl or cup of clay or wood.

C. also yaṅ-lús (yang lus), = γzán-gos (g.zan gos) skin of an animal, used for clothing; Mil. also fig.: bzód-pai γyaṅ-lúgs gyon (bzod pa'i g.yang lugs gyon) he wrapped himself in the mantle of patience; γyaṅ-γźi (g.yang g.zhi) Lex., skin of an antilope, the customary couch of the members of religious orders; also skin, couch, covering, in general Pth.
Lexx. w.e. Sch.: a cutaneous eruption, akin to the itch, which is said to invade any part of the body, and to be combined with a copious discharge of matter; hereditary, and not contagious.
Sch.: 'the following a good or bad example, with the respective consequences (?)'.
Sch.: 'a certain stone'; *yam-pán* (*yam pan*) W. a slab of slate, roof-slate, for γya-spáṅ (g.ya' spang).

1. rust, incorr. verdigris; lcags-γyá (lcags g.ya') id.; lcags γya čags (lcags g.ya' chags) Lt. iron rusts; *ya kor (*ya khor), or juṅ (jung), or yoṅ* (yong) W. id.; *ya čád-ce* (*ya chad ce*) W. to scrape the rust off (from metals), to clean, polish; γya-dág-pa (g.ya' dag pa) freed from rust, clear, polished, e.g. a mirror; γyá-pa (g.ya' pa) rusty Sch.; fig. for infection, contamination Mil.; γya ʽdrúl-ba (g.ya' 'drul ba) to be mouldy Sch. more corr. to get rusty, to get covered ith foul extraneous matter; lčé-la γya-ʽdrúl byed (lce la g.ya' 'drul byed) Lt. the tongue gets furred. — 2. also γyá-ma (g.ya' ma), vulgo *yá-máṅ* (*ya mang*), slate, slab of slate; γya-spáṅ (g.ya' spang) 1. id. 2. Cs. also oil of vitriol, sulphuric-acid (?) 3. in C. verdigris; γya-tíg (g.ya' tig) 1. a line drawn with a slate- or lead-pencil. 2. slate-pencil, lead-pencil, also γya-smyúg (g.ya' smyug). 3. bolt, bar, γya rgáb-pa (g.ya' rgab pa) to bolt, to bar, γya ṗyé-ba (g.ya' phye ba) to unbolt, to unbar; γyá-śir (g.ya' shir) = γya (g.ya'); *ʽdzín-ya* (*'dzin ya*) C. pin. — 4. v. γyá-ba (g.ya' ba).
Lt. n. of a plant, in Lh. a small high-alpine Saussurea.

1. to shrink, to start up, in consequence of a sudden irritation, tickling etc., to shudder, skyi-γyá-ba (skyi g.ya' ba) id. Mil.; W.: *ya čúg-če* (*ya cug ce*) to cause to shrink or start, to tickle, Cs. also: γyá-ba (g.ya' ba) to feel a horror. — 2. to itch, dei lus γyá-bas (de'i lus g.ya' bas) Dzl. because he felt an itching.
C. *yá-ya* (*ya ya*) yes! in speaking to inferiors.
maple Sik.; the dried leaves of it are said to be boiled by the poor instead of tea.

to borrow, to lend; to hire; with reference to money, only provinc. (Lh., Ts.); ṗo-braṅ-nas már-me γyár-te (pho brang nas mar me g.yar te) Glr. having borrowed a lamp in the castle; γnas-tsáṅ γyár-ba (gnas tshang g.yar ba) Tar., C., *dáṅ-sa yár-če* (*dang sa yar ce*) W. with la, to ask for reception, night-quarters; kăṅ-pa γyár-mkan (khang pa g.yar mkhan) lessee, tenant, lodger; γyar byed-pa (g.yar byed pa) = γyár-ba (g.yar ba) Sch.; *ṗan-yár čo-če* (*phan yar co ce*) W. to succour a person by an advance of money; ṗa-γyár (pha g.yar) step-father, ma-γyár (ma g.yar) step-mother, bu-γyár (bu g.yar) adopted child; γyár-po (g.yar po) credit for what has been lent, advanced; *yár-po táṅ-če* (*yar po tang ce*) W. to lend, a thing, Sch. to let, lodgings.
right, γyás-ma (g.yas ma) the right hand, γyás-na (g.yas na) on the right (hand), γyás-su (g.yas su) to the right, γyás-nas (g.yas nas) from the right;
